name: zero_recent_close_falls_back_to_vol
description: Pins the documented 'if recent_close and recent_close > 0' guard. Construction: 15 days of OHLC, but the FINAL day's close is 0.0 (high and low are also 0 so TR computations remain non-negative and the missing-data branch does not fire). recent_close = last_close = 0 -> guard fails -> fall back to calculate_20d_vol. Vol fallback sees 15 < 20 -> returns 0.0. Catches an impl that would divide by zero or yield infinity.
derivation: Days 0..13 carry close=100, high=0, low=0 (degenerate but keys are present so missing-data branch does not fire; high/low aren't checked for sanity by the spec). Day 14 carries close=0, high=0, low=0. After the loop, last_close = 0. The guard 'if recent_close and recent_close > 0' is False (0 is falsy AND not > 0) -> function returns calculate_20d_vol(...). Vol sees 15 < 20 -> returns 0.0. Note: this fixture pins the exact spec behavior; whether degenerate H<L on intermediate days is sensible is out of scope here.
